citation.sourceDocument: Code of Virginia §58.1-320 income-tax rates; §58.1-322.03 standard deduction & exemption (HB1600, 2025)
citation.sourceNote: Brackets — 2% to $3,000, 3% to $5,000, 5% to $17,000, 5.75% above — are the same for every filing status and unchanged since 1990 (§58.1-320). The $8,750 single / $17,500 married standard deduction applies to taxable years 2025 and 2026 under HB1600 (2025, §58.1-322.03), then reverts to $3,000 / $6,000 for 2027 and after unless the General Assembly extends it. Personal exemption is $930 per exemption — one for single filers (and federal head-of-household filers, who use Virginia's single status since Virginia has no head-of-household status), two for married filing jointly. Dependent, age-65-or-older, and blind exemptions and the optional itemized deduction are omitted, the engine's no-dependent / standard-deduction convention. Verified against tax.virginia.gov/deductions on 2026-06-06.
